title: Lent and Palm Sunday Sermons from the Middle English Filius Matris Cycle
summary: The approved General Corpus XML artifact is the full-header U-M CME90033 representation of a 2023 structured encoding. U-M obtained its text from Oxford Text Archive item 0033, preserved the OTA/Carpenter lineation, removed some OTA semantic conventions, and added structural divisions, line numbers, and folio references after comparison with Christine Thérèse Carpenter's 1984 dissertation. The OTA text is based on or closely associated with Carpenter's edition of seven Lent and Palm Sunday sermons transcribed from British Library Harley MS 2276, broadly ff. 52v-70v. The selected sermons belong to a larger fifteenth-century Middle English translation of the late-twelfth-century Latin Filius matris cycle.
review_notes: The primary transmission path is repository artifact to U-M encoding to OTA text to Carpenter dissertation edition to Harley MS 2276. U-M also consulted the dissertation directly when adding markup, so the graph includes a supporting U-M-to-dissertation relation.
CME90033 and OTA0033 are identifiers at different publication and upstream encoding layers; both are preserved as aliases without treating the artifacts as identical.

The publication override selects CME/source/recent_changes/additions_2023/CME90033.xml. The alternate CME/source/CME_from_OTA/CME90033.xml has a sparse header and different fixity, although canonicalized EEBO bodies compare byte-identically.

The broad work locus ff. 52v-70v follows U-M-added milestones. Every sermon after the first begins before its first internal folio milestone, so exact per-sermon opening loci remain partly inferred until manuscript images or Carpenter's edition can be inspected.

No public complete manuscript facsimile was found in the exact current BL record or linked legacy viewer as of 2026-07-14. This dated negative finding does not prove that no private, onsite, or unindexed reproduction exists.

Access and rights apply only to the entity and component named. They must not be inherited automatically by medieval text, manuscript images, Carpenter's edition, OTA text, U-M markup, or repository output.

entities.label: General Corpus approved source XML for CME90033
entities.description: The exact XML artifact selected by the repository's publication override and consumed by the CME90033 publication workflow. It is supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The artifact contains CME90033 in IDG/@ID and VID, while STC and BIBNO carry the upstream identifier OTA0033.
The selected file is 202,241 bytes at the pinned commit.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME90033
entities.description: The U-M structured digital encoding created in February 2023 from OTA 0033, with a new descriptive header, chapter divisions, line and folio milestones, character conversion, and selected semantic-markup changes.
notes: The current stable UMDL handle is retained even though automated access encountered a web challenge during this review; the exact encoding is preserved in the pinned CME source artifact.

entities.label: Oxford Text Archive item 0033: British Library Manuscript Harley 2276
entities.description: The public OTA plain-text encoding from which U-M says CME90033 was obtained. The file contains seven S blocks and retains compact plaintext conventions for abbreviation expansions, named entities, manuscript underlining, and special characters.

date_statements.label: current OTA record issue date
date_statements.notes: The current record's dc.date.issued value is preserved as catalog metadata, not silently reconciled with the U-M header.

date_statements.label: U-M embedded OTA publication date
date_statements.notes: The U-M SOURCEDESC supplies this date; its relationship to the current OTA issue date and the 1984 dissertation is unresolved.

notes: The OTA abstract describes one sermon for each of five Lent Sundays plus Palm Sunday, but the downloadable text has seven numbered S blocks because two sermons are associated with the First Sunday in Lent.
The cached direct download is complete for the provider's harley2276-0033.txt file, not a manuscript facsimile or dissertation scan.
The legacy citation PURL http://purl.ox.ac.uk/ota/0033 currently redirects to https://ota.ox.ac.uk/id/0033, which returned HTTP 404 on 2026-07-14; it is retained only as a historical identifier.

entities.label: Carpenter 1984 dissertation edition of the Lent and Palm Sunday sermons
entities.description: Christine Thérèse Carpenter's two-volume unpublished doctoral dissertation, including a partial edition of the English Filius matris cycle transcribed from Harley MS 2276. U-M identifies volume 1, pages 230-359, as the locus associated with its digital text.

bibliographic.citation: Christine Thérèse Carpenter, 'An Edition of the Lent and Palm Sunday Sermons Contained in the Fifteenth-Century Translation of the Late Twelfth-Century Latin Filius Matris Cycle,' 2 vols., PhD dissertation, Bedford College, University of London, 1984; selected text in vol. 1, pp. 230-359.
bibliographic.title: An Edition of the Lent and Palm Sunday Sermons Contained in the Fifteenth-Century Translation of the Late Twelfth-Century Latin Filius Matris Cycle
bibliographic.pages: vol. 1, pp. 230-359 for the selected text

notes: U-M reports that the OTA-associated text aligns with the dissertation's line breaks but omits its apparatus, folio and page references, introduction, Latin glosses and side notes, and commentary.
No complete public dissertation copy was verified during this review.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 2276
entities.description: A circa-1450 English parchment manuscript containing sixty Middle English sermons translated from the Latin Filius matris cycle at ff. 1r-146v and an alphabetical subject index at ff. 146v-148v. The CME90033 selection is mapped broadly to ff. 52v-70v by U-M-added milestones.

holding.notes: Held by the British Library in London. The catalog's Digitised Content field was blank when checked on 2026-07-14.

physical_description.extent: 1 preliminary leaf, 149 manuscript folios, and four flyleaves at both beginning and end
physical_description.dimensions: 265 x 190 mm; written space 195 x 130 mm
notes: Written in Gothic cursive script.
The full sermon collection occupies ff. 1r-146v; the alphabetical subject index occupies ff. 146v-148v.
The current catalog identifies England as origin.
The seven-sermon locus is broader than the first internal milestone of each sermon; exact opening boundaries need image or dissertation inspection.

entities.label: British Library Archives and Manuscripts catalog record for Harley MS 2276
entities.description: The current exact institutional catalog description used for shelfmark, contents, date, physical description, holding, and digitization-status evidence.

entities.label: Bennett 2021 scholarly contextual source
entities.description: A peer-reviewed article used here only as a scholarly contextual and bibliographic source for the English Filius matris translation and Carpenter dissertation, not as a transmission source for CME90033.

bibliographic.citation: Alastair Bennett, 'A Middle English Sermon Based on Gregory the Great's Homily for the Second Sunday in Advent,' Mediaeval Studies 83 (2021): 27-57.
bibliographic.title: A Middle English Sermon Based on Gregory the Great's Homily for the Second Sunday in Advent

notes: Page 29 note 7 identifies the English translation as fifteenth-century and supplies the full Carpenter dissertation reference.

subject.description: The approved full-header repository XML at the pinned CME commit

object.description: U-M Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME90033

subject.description: Seven structurally divided U-M sermon units with added line and folio milestones

object.description: Seven numbered S blocks in harley2276-0033.txt

scope.notes: U-M says it obtained the raw text from OTA 0033, retained its arbitrary line breaks, removed named-entity and abbreviation-expansion markup, and transformed other conventions.

object.description: Carpenter's edited Lent and Palm Sunday sermons

scope.notes: The U-M header deliberately describes the file as based on or at least closely associated with the dissertation: line breaks align, but major apparatus and commentary are absent.

subject.description: U-M's 2023 additions of line numbers, folio references, chapter divisions, and character interpretation

scope.notes: This is a supporting editorial relation, not an alternate primary transmission edge replacing OTA.

subject.description: The dissertation's edition of the seven Lent and Palm Sunday sermons

object.description: The corresponding sermons in the larger Harley witness

scope.notes: The manuscript locus is derived from U-M's added folio milestones; images or the dissertation are still needed to verify every sermon boundary.

object.description: The complete manuscript object, including sermon collection and index

subject.description: Discussion of fifteenth-century translations of earlier sermon cycles

object.description: Carpenter's partial edition and dissertation bibliographic identity

notes: The exact artifact is available in the pinned CME submodule checkout.
The stable item handle is authoritative in the source metadata. Automated retrieval during review encountered an access challenge; the exact XML is independently preserved in the repository artifact.

local_copies.label: Complete OTA harley2276-0033.txt download

work_portion.label: The complete provider file represents the seven-sermon selection
locators: lines 1-2251 of the cached plaintext file
notes: This is the complete OTA textual file, not the full Harley manuscript or full English sermon cycle.
Provider filename harley2276-0033.txt; exact direct download URL retained above.
The OTA record exposes the derived plain-text file, not a dissertation facsimile. No exact open full-dissertation record or download was verified in the authoritative and scholarly sources checked.

The exact current catalog record's Digitised Content field was blank, the legacy FullDisplay URL redirected to a generic digitised-manuscripts page, and no working exact public IIIF object was verified.

The desired research surrogate is the whole codex where possible, not merely ff. 52v-70v; any future order should request covers or boards, spine if available, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, ff. 1r-149, the adjacent sermon cycle, and the index.

access.access_method: Submit the British Library request form for physical access to collection items in London, citing Harley MS 2276 and record 040-002048107.

Submit the individual digitisation-order form with shelfmark Harley MS 2276, desired full-codex coverage, color specification, research-reference purpose, and any intended reuse. Request a written quote and rights guidance before ordering.
access.format: digital photography or other feasible reproduction chosen by the provider
access.contact: licensing@bl.uk (Digitisation Services page); permissions@bl.uk (January 2026 Imaging Services terms)
notes: The service page says original items may be photographed when scanning is unsuitable, color must be requested explicitly, and standard electronic delivery is available. Large full-codex orders may fall outside standard service and require a bespoke quote.
The current Digitisation Services page and the January 2026 Imaging Services terms publish different reproduction contacts; both are preserved rather than silently choosing one.
Ordering or receiving images does not itself grant publication or repository-distribution permission.

local_copies.label: Complete Bennett 2021 article PDF

work_portion.label: Filius matris translation and Carpenter dissertation citation
notes: The entire 32-page article PDF is cached; only this short contextual passage concerns CME90033's lineage.
The PDF is encrypted with provider-set copy and print restrictions; text accessibility extraction was available for research inspection.

qpdf 12.3.2 --check exited 3 after converting kid number 0 in Name/Number tree object 857 to an indirect object; it otherwise reported success with warnings. Poppler read all 32 pages, and the provider bytes were preserved without repair.

rights.component: The anonymous fifteenth-century Middle English sermon text as a historical work, excluding modern transcriptions, editions, annotations, photographs, catalog prose, and access terms
rights.basis: The underlying anonymous medieval expression predates modern copyright terms by centuries. This layer-specific assessment does not determine rights in later editorial or photographic representations.
notes: This is a cataloging assessment, not legal advice, and is deliberately limited to the historical text in the United States.

rights.component: Existing or newly ordered manuscript photographs and provider-supplied reproductions
rights.basis: No public image set was available to inspect. British Library imaging terms govern supplied reproductions and say images must not be reproduced without written permission.
notes: Ask both licensing@bl.uk (named by Digitisation Services) and permissions@bl.uk (named by the January 2026 Imaging Services terms) whether reference images may be redistributed in a public git repository and under what credit, resolution, fee, or license conditions.

rights.component: Carpenter's dissertation edition, introduction, apparatus, commentary, transcribed text presentation, and other original scholarly contributions
rights.basis: The dissertation is a modern 1984 scholarly work and no open license or rights statement for a complete copy was found.
notes: The public-domain status of the medieval text does not extinguish possible rights in Carpenter's original editorial work.

rights.component: The OTA-distributed harley2276-0033.txt digital text as offered by the provider
rights.basis: The current OTA record and U-M header state distribution under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 3.0 Unported.
notes: The license's NonCommercial condition is relevant to downstream profit-oriented print-on-demand use. This record does not resolve whether every component of the file was licensed by every possible rightsholder.

rights.component: The U-M-distributed CME90033 encoding and provider-attributed additions
rights.basis: The exact U-M header states that Oxford and consequently U-M distribute the file under CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 Unported.
notes: The header's provider assertion is recorded without assuming that it governs the medieval work, Carpenter dissertation, or future manuscript images.

rights.component: The checked-in repository artifact and repository-authored software or markup contributions, excluding upstream medieval, editorial, and photographic layers
rights.basis: The pinned CME repository LICENSE contains the Unlicense and expressly refers to software. Its scope over third-party text, upstream transcription, and data or markup in CME90033 is not explicit.
notes: Do not treat the repository software license as a clean override of the source file's CC BY-NC-SA statement or Carpenter's possible rights.

rights.component: British Library catalog text and metadata presentation
rights.basis: The current catalog footer identifies catalog text as available under a Creative Commons Attribution license.
notes: The catalog-text license does not license manuscript images.

rights.component: Bennett's article and the provider PDF
rights.jurisdiction: United States, Canada, and recipient jurisdiction
rights.basis: The PDF states copyright by the Pontifical Institute of Mediaeval Studies and exposes no open-reuse license; public download is not equivalent to redistribution permission.
notes: The cached PDF is a private, gitignored research copy and should not be committed or redistributed without permission.

editorial_practices.description: Represents only seven Lent and Palm Sunday sermons and omits the dissertation's apparatus, original folio and page references, introduction, Latin glosses and side notes, and commentary.
editorial_practices.representation: Middle English edited text only, with a newly supplied digital header and argument

editorial_practices.description: Preserves arbitrary source line breaks because they align with the dissertation and support its line-based reference system; P and LB structures therefore record editorial layout rather than verse.
editorial_practices.representation: P and LB elements plus numbered line MILESTONE elements

editorial_practices.description: Removes OTA percent-sign markup that identified expansions, leaving expanded forms silently in the U-M text.
editorial_practices.scope: all expanded abbreviations in the encoding
editorial_practices.representation: expanded letters retained without explicit expansion markup

editorial_practices.description: Marks letters supplied when expanding manuscript abbreviations with percent signs.
editorial_practices.source_feature: abbreviated manuscript forms as represented by Carpenter/OTA
editorial_practices.representation: %...% around expanded characters, for example ma%n%

editorial_practices.description: Removes OTA plus-sign named-entity notation rather than carrying it into the U-M XML.
editorial_practices.representation: entity text retained without semantic tagging

editorial_practices.description: Retains OTA markup for passages underlined in the manuscript as Q elements and slightly expands element boundaries to include adjacent punctuation.
editorial_practices.representation: Q elements with slightly expanded punctuation coverage
notes: Q is a digital editorial representation; the manuscript appearance cannot be verified without images.

editorial_practices.description: Compares the OTA-derived text with the printed dissertation and adds line numbers, folio references, chapter divisions, and contextual character-entity conversions.
editorial_practices.source_feature: OTA plaintext checked against Carpenter's dissertation
editorial_practices.representation: DIV1 divisions, line and folio MILESTONE elements, and Unicode/XML character entities

evidence.citation: Kyvero Press CME repository, source/recent_changes/additions_2023/CME90033.xml at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Exact approved source artifact. Its header, argument, editorial declaration, revision description, identifiers, seven DIV1 units, and milestones establish the U-M/OTA/dissertation/witness chain and U-M editorial practices.
Binds CME90033.pdf to the recent_changes/additions_2023 XML with SHA-256 869d4e... and records the CME_from_OTA XML only as an alternate source.

evidence.citation: General Corpus canonicalized XML body comparison, 2026-07-14.
evidence.summary: The approved recent_changes XML and alternate CME_from_OTA XML have distinct whole-file SHA-256 values and headers, but their canonicalized EEBO subtrees are byte-identical with SHA-256 9f188eebceb8c68ef23c2d285e117b8a296a528e8e638a5ca37ffab362ef94e4.
notes: Alternate file: CME/source/CME_from_OTA/CME90033.xml; SHA-256 2da84fb3c0695736ca56245b9222e890a8f047cef6e401c902e4c89e3008be00; git blob f7e2c9f04eaf90bd70643b0880a0063214b3da63.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, About the Collection.
evidence.summary: Describes the public U-M collection and its assembly of Middle English texts, including early electronic texts supplied through the Oxford Text Archive.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive, 'British Library. Manuscript. Harley 2276,' item 20.500.12024/0033, full record.
evidence.summary: Identifies ota:0033, contributor Carpenter, Middle English language, current issue date 1980-01-01, public status, CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 statement, a legacy citation PURL, and the downloadable harley2276-0033.txt bitstream. The PURL's current redirect target returned HTTP 404 on 2026-07-14.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive, harley2276-0033.txt, item 0033, sequence 3.
evidence.summary: Complete 112,063-byte provider text file. Inspection confirms seven S blocks, 2,251 physical lines, and OTA conventions later described and transformed by U-M.

evidence.citation: Alastair Bennett, 'A Middle English Sermon Based on Gregory the Great's Homily for the Second Sunday in Advent,' Mediaeval Studies 83 (2021): 27-57, especially p. 29 n. 7.
evidence.summary: States that the late-twelfth-century Latin Filius matris cycle was translated into Middle English in the fifteenth century and cites Carpenter's 1984 Bedford College, University of London dissertation as a partial edition. Poppler reads the complete 32-page cached article; qpdf exits 3 with a recoverable Name/Number-tree warning, and the encrypted provider file is retained without repair.

evidence.citation: Index of Middle English Prose, Handlist XXIV: Cambridge University Library MS Kk.6.2, Cambridge Core summary.
evidence.summary: Describes the full English translation of the late-twelfth- or early-thirteenth-century Latin Collectio Filius matris, its traditional attribution to William de Montibus, and Harley 2276 among the English witnesses.

evidence.citation: British Library, 'Middle English sermon collection,' Harley MS 2276, Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, record 040-002048107.
evidence.summary: Exact current catalog description for the circa-1450 English parchment manuscript, its sixty-sermon Filius matris translation, index, dimensions, extent, script, holding, and blank Digitised Content field.

evidence.citation: British Library legacy Digitised Manuscripts FullDisplay URL for Harley MS 2276, checked 2026-07-14.
evidence.summary: The former item-style FullDisplay URL redirected to the general digitised-manuscripts page and did not expose a working exact public facsimile during review.

evidence.citation: British Library, collection-item physical-access request form.
evidence.summary: Current request route linked for arranging onsite access to manuscripts and other collection items.

Explains ordering of images, reference versus reproduction uses, need for shelfmark and folio information, color requests, provider choice of feasible imaging method, fees, electronic delivery, and gives licensing@bl.uk as a contact.

evidence.citation: British Library, individual digitisation-order online form.
evidence.summary: Current online submission route for obtaining a quotation for imaging an individual collection item.

evidence.citation: British Library, Terms and Conditions for Imaging Services, updated January 2026.
evidence.summary: States fee and delivery terms, a standard target of 30 working days after payment, possible bespoke handling for hard or large orders, that supplied images must not be reproduced without written permission, and gives permissions@bl.uk as a contact.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME LICENSE at pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Contains the Unlicense and describes software; it does not expressly establish its scope over upstream third-party texts, scholarly editions, transcriptions, or manuscript images.

open_questions.question: What is the exact technical and bibliographic relationship among the OTA record's 1980 issue date, the U-M header's 1983 OTA date, and Carpenter's 1984 dissertation?
next_steps: Request OTA deposit/version history and inspect the complete dissertation's preliminaries and file conventions.
Until then, retain each dated assertion at its own layer and describe OTA as based on or closely associated with the dissertation.

open_questions.question: Is a complete public facsimile of Harley MS 2276 available outside the current exact BL catalog routes, or can the British Library supply a complete research-quality color surrogate?
next_steps: Ask the British Library to confirm digitization status and quote a whole-codex color order.
Specify covers or boards, spine if available, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, ff. 1r-149, adjacent sermons, and index.
Request exact reuse terms in writing.

open_questions.question: What are the exact manuscript opening and closing folios and dissertation page ranges for each sermon?
next_steps: Inspect the requested manuscript images and complete dissertation.
Treat the U-M milestones as supporting only the broad ff. 52v-70v span until the opening of sermons 2-7 can be verified directly.

open_questions.question: Can the anonymous translation's date, origin, and relationship among Harley 2276 and the other English witnesses be refined?
next_steps: Consult Carpenter's dissertation, Helen Spencer's 1977 article, and fuller Index of Middle English Prose descriptions for the English Filius matris witnesses.
Do not infer intellectual-work origin from Harley's production data.

open_questions.question: Which permissions and licenses govern redistribution of a future BL facsimile, Carpenter's dissertation edition, U-M-added markup, and the repository XML in a public or profit-supporting corpus workflow?
next_steps: Obtain written BL imaging permission for any ordered images.
Locate the dissertation rights holder or repository terms.
Ask Oxford and U-M to clarify the scope of CC BY-NC-SA 3.0.
Ask Kyvero Press whether the Unlicense is intended to cover data or markup as well as software.
notes: Noncommercial licensed source material should not be assumed compatible with profit-oriented print-on-demand redistribution merely because the underlying medieval text is public domain.
